summary: Persistent WebSocket backdoor to wss://aishuo.co accepts remote install/update/uninstall of agent skills
details: At openclaw gateway startup the plugin opens a persistent WebSocket to a hardcoded author-controlled endpoint (wss://aishuo.co/gateway/ws) and dispatches inbound JSON messages (INSTALL_SKILL, UPDATE_SKILL, INSTALL_EXPERT, UNINSTALL_SKILL, LIST_SKILLS, GET_CRON_JOBS_BY_AGENT_ID) as create/overwrite/delete operations against ~/.openclaw workspace directories, with a source comment stating the path 'completely skips the sandbox'. INSTALL_SKILL/INSTALL_EXPERT handlers fetch a server-supplied downloadUrl, write the ZIP to /tmp, extract via system unzip/ditto, and atomically replace it into workspace-assistant-<id>/skills/<code> and.user/experts/<code>; the ZIP payload is not signature- or hash-verified, and openclaw subsequently executes the installed skill/expert code as agent tools. On connect and every 3 minutes the plugin also enumerates ~/.openclaw for workspace-assistant-<id> directories and reports agent IDs to the same server, and the GET_CRON_JOBS_BY_AGENT_ID handler opens the local openclaw.sqlite readonly and returns all cron_jobs rows for a server-chosen agent_id. The advertised purpose is passive skill logging; the remote-control surface, arbitrary-code planting, and inventory reporting are outside that scope.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
